title: Supply Chain Attack - Malicious Package in Internal PyPI Mirror
mitre_technique: T1195.001 - Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
raw_evidence: 2025-07-14T16:29:05Z — CI pipeline on CI-RUNNER-07 installed requests-plus==2.31.1 from internal PyPI mirror — package not present in approved dependency manifest.
2025-07-14T16:29:07Z — Post-install hook in requests-plus setup.py executed: base64-decoded shell command spawning outbound connection to 203.0.113.88:4444.
2025-07-14T16:29:14Z — Environment variable dump transmitted: included AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN from CI environment.
2025-07-14T16:29:18Z — requests-plus package uploaded to internal mirror 47 minutes ago by service account ci_mirror_sync — account not used for 23 days prior.
2025-07-14T16:29:22Z — Legitimate package requests 2.31.0 exists; requests-plus is a typosquat with one character difference.
2025-07-14T16:30:01Z — 3 other CI runners installed the same package in the last 47 minutes: CI-RUNNER-02, CI-RUNNER-05, CI-RUNNER-09.

context.account_type: automated service account — PyPI mirror synchronisation
context.affected_pipelines: all pipelines using requests or requests-plus dependency
context.secrets_exposed: AWS credentials, GitHub token from 4 CI runners
context.asset_criticality_destination: CRITICAL — CI/CD pipeline compromise; production deployment capability at risk
